mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising CPI (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of low unemployment (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in industrial production over 3 months signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising industrial production (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts and consumer spending signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of joint increases in housing starts and consumer spending (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2% signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of low federal funds rates (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in the unemployment rate and increase in payroll employment signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5% and PAYEMS rises > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of joint declines in unemployment and increases in payroll employment (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in GDP signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising GDP (t-test p > 0.05).
